rag_rules: Keep generated text in the owner scope it was produced for: type-level contract clauses belong in component type annexes, while implementation-level bindings belong in implementation annexes.
Use the analyzed AADL model as the source of truth for valid owners and scopes.
Do not place implementation-only references, such as subcomponent paths, into a type-level annex.
generic_rules: Preserve the existing architecture during fusion; do not rename or alter components, features, subcomponents, connections, or properties unless the edit explicitly targets them.
Apply edits only within the intended owner and leave unrelated declarations untouched.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE content is wrapped as annex agree {** ... **};
Maintain balanced annex delimiters and valid AADL block termination after any insertion or merge.
anti_patterns: Do not fuse type-scope text into an implementation owner, or implementation-only text into a type owner.
Do not restructure surrounding AADL declarations just to make an annex insertion fit.
irrelevant_topics: expression typing diagnostics and helper-variable repair
numeric range comparisons over Boolean or opaque data
AADL property names treated as AGREE variables
